claims.claim: Zhipu AI was founded in 2019, spun out of Tsinghua University's Knowledge Engineering Group (KEG), by professors Tang Jie and Li Juanzi.
Zhipu's operating CEO is Zhang Peng; Tang Jie is founder and chief scientist; Liu Debing is chairman and a co-controller.
GLM introduced an autoregressive blank-infilling pretraining objective with 2D positional encodings (ACL 2022 paper).
GLM-130B (2022) was an open bilingual 130B-parameter dense model that reached INT4 quantization with almost no performance loss.
GLM-4.5 (July 2025) is Zhipu's first Mixture-of-Experts model: 355B total / 32B active parameters, trained on 23T tokens, released under the MIT license; GLM-4.5-Air is 106B/12B.
GLM-4.6 (Oct 2025) expanded context from 128K to 200K tokens and is over 30% more token-efficient than GLM-4.5.
GLM-4.5 reported 64.2% on SWE-bench Verified and 91.0% on AIME 24 in its own technical report, ranking 3rd of 14 models on the vendor's averaged benchmark.
GLM-4.6 scored 68.0% on SWE-bench Verified vs Claude Sonnet 4.5's 77.2% on the vendor's own grid; on independent LMArena its Elo (~1425) sits below frontier Western models (~1500+).

GLM-5.2 (June 2026) is a 744B-total / 40B-active MoE with a 1M-token context window, MIT-licensed; Artificial Analysis rated it the leading open-weights model at Intelligence Index 51, #4 overall behind Fable 5, Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5.

GLM-Image was trained end-to-end on Huawei Ascend Atlas 800T A2 hardware with the MindSpore framework, with no US chips; the GLM-5 LLM's own model card discloses training tokens but not training hardware.

On 16 January 2025 the US BIS added 10 Zhipu-affiliated entities to the Entity List (90 FR 4617, FR Doc 2025-00704), citing that they 'advance the People's Republic of China's military modernization through the development and integration of advanced artificial intelligence research'; the entry imposes a license requirement for all items subject to the EAR with a presumption of denial, with no footnote/military-end-user designation.

Zhipu (as Knowledge Atlas Technology) IPO'd on HKEX (ticker 2513) on 8 January 2026 at HK$116.20, raising about HK$4.35B (US$560M), closing day one at HK$131.5 (+13.17%) for a market cap of about HK$57.89B.

By late May 2026 Zhipu's HKEX shares had risen nearly 1,600% since the January listing to a market cap above HK$880B (about US$112B); a UBS analyst attributed the level to a 'scarcity premium and limited tradable shares due to lock-up periods'.
Zhipu's FY2025 revenue rose 131.9% year-on-year to ¥724M (about US$105M) while its statutory net loss widened to ¥4.72B; the adjusted net loss was about ¥3.2B and R&D spending was about ¥3.18B.

In H1 2025 about 84.8% of Zhipu's revenue came from on-premise/private deployment, with cloud API (true MaaS) only 15.2%; its blended gross margin fell from 56.3% in 2024 to 41.0% in 2025 and the cloud-API line ran a negative gross margin.
Cumulative net losses across 2022 to H1 2025 totalled roughly ¥6.2B (¥144M in 2022, ¥788M in 2023, ¥2.958B in 2024, ¥2.358B in H1 2025).
Saudi Aramco's venture arm Prosperity7 joined a ~US$400M financing in 2024 that valued Zhipu at about US$3B, making it among the first foreign backers of a major Chinese generative-AI firm.

In December 2024 Zhipu raised ¥3B (US$412M) with backers including Alibaba, Tencent and state-backed vehicles; in March 2025 it took about US$257M from local-government investors in Hangzhou, Chengdu and Zhuhai after the US blacklisting.

In June 2024 Zhipu cut GLM API prices to as low as 0.1 yuan (about US$0.014) per million-token prompt — roughly 90% below the industry average — and made GLM-4-Flash free, joining China's LLM price war.

Zhipu's GLM Coding Plan ships an Anthropic-protocol-compatible endpoint marketed as a drop-in Claude Code replacement at about 1/7th the price, with Lite/Pro/Max tiers (about $18/$72/$160 per month as of mid-2026).
Chinese models reached about 61% of token consumption on OpenRouter by early 2026; Zhipu's z-ai provider page shows GLM-5.2 at 1.29T cumulative tokens and roughly 396,000 monthly requests.

Alibaba's Qwen family captured over 50% of global open-weight model downloads in early 2026 (153.6M in February alone), and Zhipu is absent from QuestMobile's top-5 China consumer AI-app MAU ranking led by ByteDance Doubao (~345M MAU).

GLM-5 completed full-stack adaptation across seven domestic chip platforms (Huawei Ascend, Moore Threads, Hygon, Cambricon, Kunlunxin, Muxi, Enflame); GLM-4.6 reached FP8+Int4 inference deployment on Cambricon chips.
Huawei's CloudMatrix 384 (Ascend 910C) reaches GB200-NVL72-class system performance only by drawing about 4.1x the power and 2.5x worse power per FLOP.

Zhipu is pursuing a STAR Market (Shanghai) H-to-A listing targeting up to ¥15B, with Guotai Haitong and CICC as sponsors; its CSRC tutoring reached 'acceptance' status on 17 June 2026, enabled by the July 2025 expansion of STAR's pre-profit 'fifth set' listing standards.

By 30 September 2025 Zhipu reported its GLM models served over 12,000 enterprise clients and more than 45 million developers; a Ningxia branch of China Telecom was its second-largest customer in 2025 and top-five customer concentration was about 40%.

Zhipu's overseas revenue was 11.6% of total in H1 2025 (up from 0.5% in 2024), with Southeast Asia contributing ¥17.9M (about US$2.5M); it operates offices in the Middle East, UK, Singapore and Malaysia.
Zhipu raised GLM-5.1 API pricing by 83% rather than cutting it, even as DeepSeek priced its V4 model about 97% below GPT-5.5.
